edges.description: Loss of IRX5-mediated repression of SDF1/CXCL12 perturbs migratory developmental programs needed for normal craniofacial morphogenesis.
edges.target: Impaired cranial neural crest and branchial arch progenitor migration

edges.description: IRX5 deficiency disrupts osteogenic maturation programs and the mineralization of specific cranial bones.
edges.target: Impaired osteoblast maturation and cranial mineralization

edges.description: Loss of IRX5 impairs ventricular conduction programs by disrupting regulation of Nav1.5, Connexin 40, and SCN5A expression in cardiomyocytes.
Disturbed craniofacial patterning manifests as severe hypertelorism or telecanthus, brachycephaly, midface prominence, and related facial anomalies.
edges.source: Impaired cranial neural crest and branchial arch progenitor migration

edges.description: Reduced mineralization lowers bone density and predisposes to repeated fractures and long-bone fragility.
edges.source: Impaired osteoblast maturation and cranial mineralization

nodes.description: Homozygous deleterious variants impair IRX5, a developmental homeobox transcription factor that coordinates embryonic patterning across cranial, skeletal, and other organ systems.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in IRX5 impair craniofacial development and germ cell migration via SDF1.
evidence.snippet: Using homozygosity mapping and locus resequencing, we found that alterations in the homeodomain of the IRX5 transcription factor cause a recessive congenital disorder affecting face, brain, blood, heart, bone and gonad development.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Molecular Update on the Fourth Reported Family with Hamamy Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Whole-exome sequencing studies performed on the 2 affected individuals and one obligate carrier revealed the presence of a homozygous c.503G>A (p.Arg168His) missense mutation in IRX5 in both sibs, not reported in any other family.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in IRX5 impair craniofacial development and germ cell migration via SDF1.
evidence.snippet: Using homozygosity mapping and locus resequencing, we found that alterations in the homeodomain of the IRX5 transcription factor cause a recessive congenital disorder affecting face, brain, blood, heart, bone and gonad development.

nodes.description: Aberrant embryonic migration in craniofacial progenitor populations disrupts facial patterning and contributes to the distinctive craniofacial anomaly spectrum.
nodes.id: Impaired cranial neural crest and branchial arch progenitor migration

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in IRX5 impair craniofacial development and germ cell migration via SDF1.
evidence.snippet: We found through in vivo modeling in Xenopus laevis embryos that Irx5 modulates the migration of progenitor cell populations in branchial arches and gonads by repressing Sdf1.

nodes.description: IRX5 loss impairs osteogenic differentiation and later mineralization programs, especially in cranial bones, producing a low-mineralization / bone-fragility phenotype.
nodes.id: Impaired osteoblast maturation and cranial mineralization

evidence.reference_title: IRX3 and IRX5 Inhibit Adipogenic Differentiation of Hypertrophic Chondrocytes and Promote Osteogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Irx3 and Irx5 null mutants display severe bone deficiency in newborn and adult stages.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of Iroquois homeobox transcription factors 3 and 5 in osteoblasts disrupts cranial mineralization.
evidence.snippet: Our findings indicate that IRX3 and IRX5 can work together to regulate mineralization of specific cranial bones.
